page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the r6gd.large delivers moderate single-core performance but exhibits limitations under multi-core and memory-intensive workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scores 1519.49, placing it in the average tier, whereas the multi-core score of 3012.08 is categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests show 117.2 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 36.85 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In application-level benchmarks, the instance achieves 2.48 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 2.39 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6gd.large leverages the ARM64 architecture to offer a high memory density and local NVMe storage at a lower resource cost compared to traditional x86 alternatives. However, the tradeoff lies in its lower raw computational throughput, as evidenced by poor scores in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and LLM inference tests. This profile makes the instance suitable for memory-bound workloads that benefit from local scratch space, such as medium-sized caching layers, database replicas, and lightweight data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1519.49, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the weak and poor categories. The instance achieves 2.48 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, highlighting its utility for memory-intensive applications. The combination of high memory density and local NVMe storage offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring local scratch space. It is best suited for memory-bound applications, database caching, and lightweight data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated Graviton cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-bound workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and integrated NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated cores
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
Includes a bundled 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Delivers 2.48 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for memory-bound workloads, database caching, and lightweight data processing
